    Multipacting: an RF gun¶

    The same cav.multipacting flow on a VHF-type RF gun. Multipacting is a cavity-wall phenomenon, driven by the accelerating-mode field on the walls, so the gun is used bare here — a beam pipe only matters for a wakefield run (where ABCI needs a pipe at each end); see Wakefield on other geometries.

    import os, tempfile
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from cavsim2d import RFGun
from cavsim2d.utils.style import apply_style
apply_style()

EPKS = list(1e6 * np.linspace(1, 50, 20))     # peak fields to sweep [V/m]
PHIS = list(np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 36))    # launch phases

    The cavity¶

    gun = {'geometry': {'y1': 1.5e-2, 'R2': 3e-2, 'T2': np.deg2rad(45), 'L3': 24e-2,
                    'R4': 5e-2, 'L5': 11e-2, 'R6': 6e-2, 'L7': 19e-2, 'R8': 4e-2,
                    'T9': np.deg2rad(8), 'R10': 3e-2, 'T10': np.deg2rad(40),
                    'L11': 5e-2, 'R12': 3e-2, 'L13': 3e-2, 'R14': 3e-2, 'x': 1e-2}}
rfgun = RFGun(gun); rfgun.name = 'rfgun'
rfgun.set_workspace(os.path.join(tempfile.mkdtemp(), 'rfgun_mp'))
rfgun.plot('geometry')

    <Axes: xlabel='$z$ [mm]', ylabel='$r$ [mm]'>

    Field, launch sites and sweep¶

    rfgun.multipacting.set_mesh_parameters(h=8, p=2, pec_maxh=2)
rfgun.multipacting.compute_field()
print('modes [MHz]:', [round(f, 2) for f in rfgun.multipacting.field_frequencies])

    modes [MHz]: [213.09, 556.52, 588.02, 727.98, 837.92]

    rfgun.multipacting.show_field('E')

    BaseWebGuiScene

    rfgun.multipacting.set_emission_points([-0.15, 0.0], n_points=60)
rfgun.multipacting.show_emission_points()

    <Axes: xlabel='z [mm]', ylabel='r [mm]'>

    rfgun.multipacting.run({'proc_count': 6, 'epks': EPKS, 'phis': PHIS})
mp = rfgun.multipacting
ax = mp.plot_counter()
ax.set_title(f'RF gun - peak c20/c0 = {mp.counter.max():.2f}')
plt.show()

    Multipacting Epk sweep: 20 points on 6 processes.

    Multipacting sweep done: 20 field levels, 6516 launch sites x phases, 413.4s (20.67s per field level).
